title: quercus toumeyi
intro: Quercus toumeyi, also known as Toumey oak, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the beech family (Fagaceae) and is prized for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects. The bark, leaves, and roots of the plant have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. Quercus toumeyi can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system.
